description: Using `not` in prefix position as a boolean negation operator fires E-TYPE-045 (§42.10). `if (not @ready)` treats `not` as a negation operator, but `not` is the absence VALUE, not an operator — use `!expr` for boolean negation or `expr is not` for absence. The lowering choke-point reports E-TYPE-045. Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §42.10 `not` is the absence value, never a prefix negation operator; a prefix `not expr` in any expression position is a compile error (E-TYPE-045), steering to `!expr` (negation) or `expr is not` (absence check).
